data.title: Aave Launches Horizon to Boost Real-World Asset Adoption in DeFi
data.description:
TL;DR Bridging TradFi and DeFi: Aave Labs launches Horizon to integrate tokenized real-world assets into the DeFi ecosystem, linking traditional finance with decentralized solutions. Unlocking Stablecoin Liquidity: Horizon enables institutions to use tokenized money market funds as collateral to borrow stablecoins like USDC and GHO, reducing costs and enhancing 24/7 liquidity. Driving...
source: https://crypto-economy.com/aave-launches-horizon-to-boost-real-world-asset-adoption-in-defi/

data.title: Trump criticizes Wall Street Journal: I don't know what they are doing or saying
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Trump criticized the Wall Street Journal on Truth Social. He said: "I don't know what the Wall Street Journal is doing or saying. Their (Wall Street Journal) ideas are old and weak, which is very unfavorable to the United States. But don't be afraid, we will win everything!!!" 
Previously, the Wall Street Journal published an article revealing that the Trump family is negotiating to acquire a stake in Binance.US. However, Trump's content on Truth Social was suspected to be published a few minutes before the Wall Street Journal article was published. It is not certain whether Trump knew in advance that the Wall Street Journal would publish the relevant article.

data.title: Coinbase International will launch REZ, GRASS and RARE perpetual contracts
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Coinbase International Station announced that it will add support for Renzo, Grass and SuperRare perpetual contracts. The REZ-PERP, GRASS-PERP and RARE-PERP markets will be opened on or after 9:30 am (UTC time) on March 20, 2025.

data.title: In the past 4 hours, the entire network has liquidated 62.4094 million US dollars, mainly long orders
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily reported that Coinglass data showed that in the past 4 hours, the entire network had a liquidation of 62.4094 million US dollars, of which long positions had a liquidation of 37.1561 million US dollars and short positions had a liquidation of 25.2533 million US dollars.

data.title: Bitcoin Experiences Notable Decline Below 81,000 USDT
data.description: According to Foresight News, Bitcoin has fallen below the 81,000 USDT mark, currently trading at 80,995.71 USDT. This represents a daily decrease of 3.18%.

data.title: Lit Protocol announces LITKEY token economics, ecosystem incentives account for 41.1%
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Crypto network Lit Protocol announced the LITKEY token economics, with ecosystem airdrops accounting for 4.8%, investors accounting for 16%, public sales accounting for 6%, private sales accounting for 3%, development teams accounting for 29.1%, and ecosystem incentives accounting for 41.1%.
According to previous news, Lit Protocol completed a US$13 million Series A financing in 2022, led by 1kx, and participated by 6thManVentures, A Capital, Eniac Ventures, Figment, Filecoin, LAO, Lattice Capital, OpenSea Ventures, Slow Ventures, Spaceship, Valhalla Ventures, Village Global and Visary Capital.

data.title: U.S. House Moves to Repeal IRS Rule on DeFi Platforms
data.description:
According to ShibDaily, the U.S. House of Representatives has voted to repeal the IRS DeFi broker rule, a regulation that would have required decentralized finance (DeFi) platforms to report user transactions to the Internal Revenue Service (IRS). The House approved the measure with a 292-132 vote, showcasing bipartisan support for overturning the rule. While opposition primarily came from Democratic lawmakers, 76 Democrats joined Republicans in favor of the repeal. This decision follows the U.S. Senate's approval of the repeal on March 4, with a 70-27 vote, indicating widespread bipartisan agreement.

The IRS DeFi broker rule, if implemented, would mandate DeFi platforms, including decentralized exchanges, to report gross proceeds from cryptocurrency sales and provide taxpayer information related to these transactions. Critics argue that the decentralized nature of these platforms makes compliance with such reporting requirements challenging. Republican Representative Mike Carey, who submitted the repeal motion, stated that the rule invades the privacy of millions of Americans and hinders the development of the DeFi industry in the United States. House Financial Services Committee Chairman French Hill also praised the repeal, asserting that the IRS rule was detrimental to innovation within the digital asset industry. Hill described the rule as government overreach that could push American digital asset development overseas.

The resolution must undergo another vote in the Senate before reaching U.S. President Donald Trump, who has indicated his approval of the repeal. This effort highlights the ongoing debate over how to regulate the digital asset industry effectively without stifling its growth or compromising user privacy. As the industry continues to evolve, policymakers are tasked with finding a balance between regulatory oversight and fostering innovation. The repeal of the IRS DeFi broker rule underscores the challenges faced by lawmakers in navigating the complexities of digital asset regulation.
